replies.text:
​@WetSocksXL Well that depends if it is ACTUALLY a review bomb or if it is just a massive wave of negative reviews from a bunch of people trying your game all at once. 
I love how any big wave of negative reviews gets labeled as a review bomb when that is, by definition, not what a review bomb is at all. It is a coordinated effort usually directed by a content creator or group to protest against the creator of the game rather than judge the game itself. 

People said Highguard got review bombed and it didn't. It was the hot controversial game of the month and so people tried it out to the tune of over 100k players. Most people disliked the game and steam db numbers prove it. You can cry "they review bombed my game and now it's bricked!!"  but at the end of the day there was 100k players the first day and not even 24 hours later that player count dropped to under 10k. That doesn't happen from Steam reviews. The majority of people who play games don't leave reviews. They play a game for 1-2 hours max and if they don't like it they uninstall and refund it within Steam's 2 hour refund window. Now Highguard was free but the point still is that most people don't want to waste the handful of hours of free time they have to quote "give a game a real try" as these devs love to say. It is not hard at all to tell if a game is fun to you within the first 30 minutes to an hour. Hell I can look at the trailer of most games and know 100% if I'd enjoy it or not.

replies.text:
​@SinnerBeforeGod What law is this? This sounds entirely made up. So if a guy refuses to ID himself and the cop moves to detain him, and then the guy suddenly says "hey I have ID I'm a citizen", the officer now has to stop and give him another chance? That is ridiculous. We have seen plenty of videos of how these people operate. They refuse to ID themselves and scream that they are citizens and will now ID themselves when officers detain them, and then attempt to delay them by playing the same game again and again. They have also clearly said that their purpose is to waste officers' time and prevent them from doing their job. 

And there are plenty of reasons for ICE officers to arrest citizens. If citizens get in their way and attempt to stop them carrying out their lawful duties, they can get arrested regardless of citizenship. ICE are still LEOs. If a Citizen refuses to ID himself and the officer has probable cause that he is not here legally, he can get arrested until they find out who he is. 

If all this was illegal, there would be a lot of very troublesome lawsuits and attempts from various judges to stop this so called illegal behavior. But ICE are still doing their jobs, so clearly its not illegal.

Its one thing to be opposed to an oppressive state and the way it carries out law enforcement. I have a lot of complaints about that. I think No Knock warrants should be done with, and I think it is absolutely insane that cops can destroy your property in the course of their duty and not have to compensate you for it. I think warrants to search people's homes are given way too easily and judges are not held accountable for giving warrants on insufficient evidence. I think cops are not held to account when they make up false reports to get a warrant or overstate their evidence. 

But this? nah. ICE is doing an important duty- getting rid of criminals who are here illegally. The crimes of these people runs the gamut from drug dealing to sexual assault of minors. And if the cost of enforcing that is that some recalcitrant citizens have to put up with spending a night in jail, I think that's fine, as long as the cops had probable cause to make the arrest.

"Vaccines make people immune to viruses, I don't think forcing people to take them is bad just because of side effects" 

Except the Vaccines they came out with did not promise to make you immune to the virus. It only gave you a good chance at resisting an infection and supposedly made the outcomes better. But the difference between this Vaccine and every other is that other Vaccines took years, sometimes decades, before we decided they were safe enough for people to use. The Covid Vaccine was rushed, and even worse, the government gave these companies protection from being sued for side effects because they knew this was risky. 

The consequence is that a lot of people have wound up with various side effects like cardiomyopathy and these companies that made a killing off all those deaths are largely protected from gigantic lawsuits.

replies.author: @Zyart
replies.text: The only people that need a game to have BIG NUMBER are the corpos who want paid. What do I care that my single player game isn't played by millions of people? Sure, there's a slightly different discussion when it comes to multiplayer games, since those need a community to play them, but even then, it's less about the number itself, and more about the population trend. Game starts with a massive amount of people, and drops a fair amount of them in the first week? Not really a data point to care about. Every game has the initial "What's this?" period. Now, if you're past that initial drop off, and your numbers are rapidly declining instead of leveling off? Yeah. That's a valid time to think there's something fundamentally wrong with the game.
